LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS C & J LEASING, INC. AND ROYAL TAXI, INC.'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION DENIED. THE LEASE DOES NOT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST DEFENDANT'S TRANSFER OF THE NEW MEDALLION. THE LEASE IS CLEARLY FOR USE OF THE PERMIT/MEDALLION 	063, NOT #1381. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS MAY HAVE A CLAIM THAT DEFENDANT BREACHED THE LEASE SINCE DEFENDANT DID NOT MAINTAIN MEDALLION 	063 IN GOOD STANDING, THE LEASE IS SILENT AS TO ANY CHANGE IN THE MEDALLION. PLAINTIFFS ALSO FAIL TO SHOW INADEQUATE LEGAL REMEDY. THE LEASE AWARDS MONEY DAMAGES FOR REVOCATION OF THE MEDALLION, AND IF PLAINTIFF PREVAILS, LOST REVENUE IS SUBJECT TO CALCULATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aaron Hancock, Esq. of Bishop Barry Drath, Tel. 5110.596.0888, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. Plaintiff, not having complied with the 12/24/12 court order, requested the court to continue the hearing on the ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE and the MOTION TO CONFORM TO SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO A JUDGMENT. For good cause shown and to the satisfaction of the court, the MOTION TO CONFORM TO SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO A JUDGMENT will be heard on February 11, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. The ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE is ordered off calendar and the Temporary Restraining Order, which is to expire today, is not re-issued. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Plaintiffs C & J Leasing, Inc. And Royal Taxi, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 09/24/09)
Off Calendar Oct-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs C & J Leasing, Inc. And Royal Taxi, Inc.'s Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction.
APPEARANCES: Aaron Hancock, Esq. of Sellar Hazard Manning Ficenec & Lai 925/938-1430 for pltfs & moving parties; Alexander Volchegursky, Esq. of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP 392-2560 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION DENIED. THE LEASE DOES NOT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST DEFENDANT'S TRANSFER OF THE NEW MEDALLION. THE LEASE IS CLEARLY FOR USE OF THE PERMIT/MEDALLION 063, NOT #1381. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS MAY HAVE A CLAIM THAT DEFENDANT BREACHED THE LEASE SINCE DEFENDANT DID NOT MAINTAIN MEDALLION 063 IN GOOD STANDING, THE LEASE IS SILENT AS TO ANY CHANGE IN THE MEDALLION. PLAINTIFFS ALSO FAIL TO SHOW INADEQUATE LEGAL REMEDY. THE LEASE AWARDS MONEY DAMAGES FOR REVOCATION OF THE MEDALLION, AND IF PLAINTIFF PREVAILS, LOST REVENUE IS SUBJECT TO CALCULATION. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiffs C & J Leasing, Inc. And Royal Taxi, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 09/24/09)
Off Calendar Oct-13-2009 Continued To Oct-29-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS C&J LEASING, INC. AND ROYAL TAXI, INC.'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.
APPEARANCES: Aaron Hancock, Esq. of Sellar Hazard Manning Ficenec & Lai 925/938-1430 for pltfs & moving parties; Alexander Volchegursky, Esq. of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP 392-2560 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: The Court vacated its tentative ruling and continued the matter to 10/29/09 for defendant to file timely opposition. (Defendant, previously in propria persona, had filed his opposition on the reply due date. Defendant is now represented by counsel, who substituted in this morning.) Opposition now due 10/20/09 & reply due 10/27/09. TRO to remain in effect. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
